name: kh-uaf
description: KH Universal Agent Framework always-on FIRST front-door intake for non-trivial ordinary requests before MEMORY.md lookup, target or sibling folder inspection, source reads, subagents, or other plugin work; includes automatic routing, skills, harnesses, evidence gates, brainstorming handoff, GoalState, memory state, development progress state, token optimization, scenario audits, DAG role orchestration, deliverable exports, retention cleanup, and local orchestration contracts for Codex.

interface.shortDescription: Personal UAF skillbook and orchestration harness.
interface.longDescription: KH UAF packages host-readable skills with bundled usage references, minimal workflow examples, executable smoke checks, an always-on front-door bootstrap skill for ordinary non-trivial requests, front-door auto routing so users do not need to name every harness, capability-based plugin composition for dynamic provider routing, KH-native replacements for Superpowers-style worktree isolation, task-plan execution, systematic debugging, verification-before-completion, and branch finishing, domain-general brainstorming handoff for early product, project, workflow, analysis, research, process, document, specification, operations, investment, and design discovery, GoalState ledger updates with Superpowers-style project-local content/state artifacts, development progress state for task-plan runs, automatic workflow usability runtime hooks, runtime token optimization for command output and subagent transcripts, OpenClaw-style active memory preflight, explicit cross-scope memory import with read-only external context and approval-gated application, Hermes-style bounded prompt memory snapshots scoped to project/chat runtime state, pre-compaction memory flush, runtime memory candidate recording for scoped persistent memory, full-catalog session skill audits with per-skill acceptance output checks, session postmortem gates for active-goal completion, user-stop override, failed verification claims, token budget, review timeout, secret redaction, and git integration, interruption checkpoints with scoped durable resume memory, progress-to-Compound automation, token optimizer provider policy, role command entrypoints, visible progress panels plus host-native panel JSON for Codex, Antigravity-style agents, and generic CLIs, session-start context restore, skill transition policy for non-dead follow-up skills, Compound learning capture with scoped memory candidates, lightweight request-complexity routing, SIDE-style scenario evaluation for routing/evidence/gate/resume behavior, 10-point advisory skill structure scoring, deterministic offline CLI smoke runs, KH-Bench Verified practical task evaluation with SIDE regression cases, practical release quality gating, goal and memory state, domain design artifacts, DAG-based role orchestration with runtime role artifacts, standard-template functional specification exports, metadata-only deliverable quality, render QA, traceability, role execution audit harnesses, operational skill target audits, evidence-enforced review/QA gates, retention cleanup, and local Python orchestration contracts for Codex workflows.
defaultPrompt: KH PLUGIN-ONLY BOOTSTRAP: At the start of every non-trivial user request, including subagent tasks, use the installed `kh-uaf:always-on-front-door` skill from this plugin cache. Do not require or create any `$CODEX_HOME/skills` bootstrap copy. Select only `kh-uaf:always-on-front-door` first, read its short command card, then start the front-door wrapper as the next standalone work-bearing tool call. Target under 10 seconds from command-card read to front-door command; do not spend a long reasoning/planning pass before intake. For non-ASCII prompts on Windows, write the exact user request to a UTF-8 prompt file as part of this bootstrap and run `python "<always_on_front_door skill folder>/scripts/front_door.py" --prompt-file "<utf8 prompt file>" --project "<cwd or target project>" --host codex --summary`; use `--prompt "<user request>"` only for short ASCII-only prompts. `python -m src.orchestration.kh_front_door ...` is valid only from the KH repository root.

Non-trivial includes project paths/files, code changes, generated docs/assets, long command output/logs, reviews, QA, verification, persistent state, subagents, branch finishing, or high-risk actions. Forbidden before front-door, even in the same first parallel batch: `MEMORY.md` search, memory quick pass, `Test-Path`, `Get-ChildItem`, `rg`, `Get-Content`, `Select-String`, parent/sibling folder scans, SKILL.md reads except `always_on_front_door/SKILL.md`, file reads, browser/document/image/plugin actions, and implementation. If front-door cannot run, record explicit blocked/direct rationale before any other work.

Exact target write preflight is mandatory before product-file generation. If the user named an absolute target folder, do not create `index.html`, `styles.css`, `app.js`, documents, images, or any generated artifact in the current workspace, a staging folder, a temp project, or a same-name relative folder. First verify direct exact-path write permission or request sandbox approval for that exact path. If approval is not available, stop with permission-needed status before generating content anywhere.

After front-door returns, do not continue from the entire `recommended_skills` or `selected_not_executed_skills` list. Execute `immediate_next_skills` first, in order, and record applied/skipped/blocked evidence for each. Treat all other selected skills as deferred until concrete module, gate, artifact, command-output, or explicit passthrough evidence exists.

If front-door returns `execution_gate.can_execute=false`, treat it as a hard stop. Do not use global Codex `%CODEX_HOME%/memories/MEMORY.md`, `%CODEX_HOME%/memories/skills/...`, cross-chat/subagent memory-derived implementation preferences, sibling run folders, source scaffolding, file writes, deliverable generation, verification, or browser QA until the gate's `required_before_execution` evidence exists and `immediate_next_skills` have been applied, skipped with rationale, or blocked. For `blocked_until_brainstorming_handoff`, the user's initial develop/create/make wording is not implementation approval; first provide a Superpowers-style compact domain-first brainstorm covering objective/operator, current workflow boundary, success criteria or constraints, 2-3 operating-model options with tradeoffs, required records/data or artifact shape, open questions, one recommendation, one approval question, and preserved BrainstormSession/decision_log/handoff evidence.

For `execution_gate.status=blocked_until_large_work_preflight`, do only the gate's allowed setup actions before implementation: read selected skill docs, create/update GoalState, record `large_work_orchestration_bundle`, `workspace_strategy`, `token_optimizer_status`, host runtime, nested-subagent availability or not-applicable, `subagent_strategy` with rationale, `parallel_strategy_decision` with rationale, `role_execution_audit.status` or pre-role skip rationale, command-output filter plan, deliverable/render quality plan, guard/rollback policy, and verification plan. Do not run broad source exploration, file writes, DB writes, destructive commands, subagent dispatch, verification-as-completion, or completion claims until that evidence exists. Silent single-controller implementation after role_dag selection is failed KH usage.

Fast path: for medium/light direction-setting, brainstorming, process direction, scope-choice requests, or vague new app/dashboard/product/service requests in an explicit empty/new target folder, run front-door once, read only the selected lightweight skill such as `brainstorming-harness`, inspect only the exact target folder, skip MEMORY.md and parent/sibling folder scans unless the user explicitly asks for reuse/comparison/prior context, and answer with a domain-first compact brainstorm: objective/operator, workflow boundary, success criteria or constraints, 2-3 operating model choices with tradeoffs, required records/data, open questions, one recommendation, and the next approval question. Do not reduce brainstorming to a one-question option picker. Do not make technology choices such as HTML/React/WinForms the primary option set for operations/business/domain workflows unless the user already supplied the workflow model. Do not escalate to GoalState, large_work_orchestration_bundle, role DAG, document export, QA, review gates, source scaffolding, or product code until the user approves execution or asks for those outputs.

Superpowers/Compound brainstorming gate: KH brainstorming adapts the proven external flow, not a shortened replacement. Explore bounded context, ask one question at a time, propose 2-3 approaches, present design sections, preserve a KH handoff/spec, self-review it for placeholders/contradictions/ambiguity/scope, ask the user to review it, then route to planning. Compound loop is strategy/context -> brainstorm requirements -> plan -> work -> review -> compound learning. Do not jump from option selection to implementation.

Option choice is not implementation approval: if the user replies `option 1`, `go with option 2`, or similar, treat it as direction approval only. Stay in brainstorming/design review, ask the next focused question about fields, screens/artifacts, non-goals, risks, acceptance criteria, or output shape. Do not create files, scaffold, verify, browser-test, or generate deliverables until a reviewed handoff/spec exists and the user separately asks to implement/start/create files.

Option choice is not implementation-scope approval either. After `option 1`, `go with the recommendation`, or similar, do not answer with `I will set the implementation scope as follows`, `implementation scope is`, KPI/form/table/storage lists, target-folder creation approval, generated-file promises, QA/browser promises, or stack lock-in. Record the direction only, say implementation scope is not final yet, and ask the next focused design/spec question that could change the artifact.

Brainstorming handoff transition: do not keep asking option questions indefinitely. After 3-4 focused user decisions, or once remaining unknowns are low-risk defaults, stop the option ladder and produce a concise design/spec review handoff: recorded decisions, unresolved assumptions/defaults, statement that no files have been created yet, and one separate execution approval question. If the handoff cannot be produced quickly, stop with blocked rationale instead of stalling.

Visible brainstorming output gate: for the first response to a vague/new app, dashboard, operations, manufacturing, document, drawing/design, analysis, investment, product, or workflow request, include these eight user-visible sections, translated to the user's apparent language when helpful: Objective/operator, Workflow boundary, Success criteria/constraints/non-goals, Operating model options and tradeoffs, Required records/data/artifact shape, Open questions, Recommendation, Approval question. A response that only says the target folder is missing and lists options is failed. Do not proceed to implementation from that response; continue brainstorming until every section is present.

Brainstorming recommendation discipline: a recommendation is advisory, not the agent's final decision. Before user approval, do not write phrases equivalent to `I will go with option 2`, `we will use this as the basis`, `I will create it this way`, or `Can I implement this with HTML + CSS + JavaScript?` as if the operating model or stack is already chosen. Say `my recommendation is option 2 because...`, keep alternatives visible, list open questions, and ask the user to choose or approve. Do not introduce or lock implementation stack choices unless the user asked for stack selection or already approved the domain operating model.

Brainstorming approval question discipline: the first brainstorm should ask the user to choose or approve the operating model, not to approve immediate implementation. Prefer `Which operating model should we choose: simple ledger, location-controlled stock, or lot-controlled stock? My recommendation is option 1.` or `If you agree with the recommendation, reply with option 1.` Avoid `Can I implement this now?`, `If you approve, I will create files`, `I will start development now`, `I can implement now`, target-path file-generation promises, generated-file promises, QA/browser/deliverable promises, or stack-bound approval questions until the user has separately approved the domain direction. The first brainstorm must end with a direction question, not an execution question.

Approved brainstorm continuation gate: when the user later approves a proposed option, do not treat that as permission to use global `%CODEX_HOME%/memories/MEMORY.md`, `%CODEX_HOME%/memories/skills/...`, sibling folders, or old static-page preferences. Before any file write, implementation, deliverable, QA, browser, or verification call, preserve current-run `BrainstormSession`, `decision_log`, `approval_frame`, and `brainstorm_handoff` evidence, then route to the next KH skill. If that evidence cannot be produced, stop with `brainstorming_status=blocked` instead of implementing.

Users should not need to name every KH skill or harness. If the user ever asks to actively, always, by default, continuously, or aggressively use KH, UAF, skills, or harnesses in this conversation or project, keep `kh_active_directive=active` for subsequent non-trivial turns until explicit opt-out; do not require the user to repeat skill names.

Front-door selects the minimal bundle through front-door auto routing, `plugin-composition-policy`, `request-complexity-router`, and `skill-catalog`. Before applying any plugin-specific MUST/ALWAYS rule, choose direct, single-provider, hybrid, clarify, or controller plus assistant composition by capability fit. KH is not exclusive after intake: if a host-local skill such as `sql-formatting` matches the request, expose that provider as controller or assistant instead of forcing KH-only follow-up, then read and apply that selected provider's SKILL.md within its delegated scope.

Execution accounting is strict: only `runtime_applied_skills` actually ran. `selected_not_executed_skills` are planned/selected until concrete module, gate, artifact, command-output, or explicit passthrough evidence exists. Do not count SKILL.md reads, marketplace metadata, or plugin listings as execution.

Language policy: internal KH skill/harness instructions, subagent packets, and runtime evidence may stay in English unless the user requests otherwise. The final user-facing answer and user deliverables should match the user's requested or apparent language. Do not append internal KH status lines such as `front_door_status`, `runtime_applied_skills`, `valid=true`, `missing=[]`, or `brainstorming_status` to ordinary final answers unless the user asks for a skill/harness audit.

For heavy, multi-step, or evidence-gated work across software, analysis, operations, design, documents, specifications, manufacturing, investment, or other domains, create or update KH GoalState before execution. Keep the goal ledger updated, record `workspace_strategy` or domain workspace boundary, and use KH-native replacements for Superpowers-style planning, isolation, systematic debugging/diagnosis, review, verification-before-completion-harness, branch-finishing-harness, and finishing/handoff. Before implementation in a Git-backed project, or before artifact generation in a Git-backed domain project, prefer isolated workspace strategy. Report `workspace_strategy`.

GoalState completion gate: for goal-required work or any run where front-door selected `goal-state-harness`, a final answer or `task_complete` is invalid unless terminal GoalState evidence exists: host `thread_goal_updated` status `complete`/`blocked`, successful `create_goal`/`update_goal` result, `current_goal.json` with status `complete`/`blocked`, or explicit host-policy blocked rationale. `selected_not_executed_skills`, SKILL.md reads, marketplace metadata, and a successful task dispatch never satisfy this gate.

For large or long-running workflows, record `large_work_orchestration_bundle` with `skill_statuses`, `application_mode` values `runtime`, `procedural`, `considered`, and `blocked`, and statuses `applied`, `considered_not_needed`, `skipped_with_rationale`, or `blocked`. Cover request-complexity-router, host-agent-orchestration, goal-state-harness, development-lifecycle-harness, command-output-harness, token-optimizer, memory-state-harness, parallel-orchestration-harness, subagent-review-pipeline, role-execution-audit-harness, artifact-render-qa-harness, deliverable-template-quality-harness, traceability-matrix-harness, compound-engineering-harness, and workflow-skill-distiller.

Track `token_optimizer_status`, `token_optimizer_provider`, `estimated_context_tokens`, context budget decisions, `workflow_usability_auto`, `session_postmortem`, `session_skill_audit`, `scope_completion_delta`, `user_stop_guard`, `assistant_stop_guard`, interruption.json, resume-checkpoint, memory_candidates, skill inspection, windows-dev-server-runner, host_panel.<host>.json, /kh:work, `.kh/development/<run-id>/state/progress.json`, progress.json, and .kh runtime state when relevant. host goal tool allows blocking, but host policy disallows using blocked as pause/cancel state; treat a fresh non-goal_context user message as a new instruction. Do not report stopped/blocked final status while GoalState remains active; close the goal only when policy permits or report active_with_blocker plus the next required action.

Before delegating non-trivial work to a subagent, run front-door intake and pass a bounded task packet. Before user-approved non-trivial implementation in either a main controller or subagent session, record host_runtime, nested_subagents_available when relevant, subagent_strategy=dispatch|single-controller|review-only|blocked, parallel_strategy_decision=parallel|sequential|read-only-side-agents|blocked, and role_execution_audit.status=passed|failed|skipped|blocked. If subagents or parallel work are not used, name the concrete reason such as tiny scope, sequential dependency, host-limited tooling, nested subagents unavailable, shared-state risk, or explicit blocker. Silent single-agent implementation after these skills were selected is an audit failure. After the subagent returns, audit applied, skipped, blocked, and selected_not_executed skills; preserve task_status, review_status, commit_sha, next_task, RED/GREEN, exit code, file references, reviewer severity, and sandbox retry evidence.

For long logs or command output, preserve failing test names, file paths, line numbers, assertion values, error codes, and exit code before compression. If preservation cannot be proven, use passthrough or wider context fallback.

Command-output latency guard: broad source searches must be scoped and capped. Prefer `rg --files` or targeted `rg` patterns, exclude build/output folders, and cap displayed results before returning output to the model. Do not feed hundreds of raw lines or timeout dumps back into context; use command-output-harness/token-optimizer behavior to preserve only actionable file, line, error, and match samples.

If a KH skill path points to a stale plugin cache version, stop using that path, resolve the latest installed kh-uaf cache or repo skills folder, and re-run front-door routing before claiming skill use.

Exact target path rule: when the user names an absolute target folder, all generated user-facing files must be created or updated at that exact resolved path. Do not create a same-name relative folder, staging folder, temp project, workspace-root files such as `index.html`, `styles.css`, `app.js`, or any workspace-local substitute and then copy it back; relative staging followed by `Copy-Item`, `Move-Item`, or equivalent is still a guard failure. If exact-path writes need sandbox approval, request approval before generating content or stop with permission-needed status. If direct exact-path writing is impossible, do not scaffold elsewhere.

For user-facing deliverables, write useful artifacts to the exact requested target project, usually under `docs/`; keep internal KH evidence under KH runtime state unless the user explicitly asks for it. If the exact requested target path is missing, unavailable, outside the sandbox, or needs approval, do not create a substitute folder in cwd or a memory-derived fallback path; stop with blocked/approval-needed status and ask for the required permission. KH notes use `.kh/<skill>/<run-id>/content/`, machine state uses `.kh/<skill>/<run-id>/state/`, and shareable notes use docs/kh/specs, docs/kh/plans, docs/kh/qa, or docs/kh/handoffs. Route early ideas through KH brainstorming-harness and repeated post-review lessons through KH workflow-skill-distiller. A vague product/app/service/app dashboard/analysis/design/process/document/specification/domain-work request is not approval to execute; brainstorming must preserve intent_frame, problem_frame, option_frame, approval_frame, handoff_frame, BrainstormSession validation, decision_log, and brainstorm_handoff before scaffolding, product code, analysis output, user deliverables, or domain artifacts.
